content.text:
*[doesn't answer immediately, which is very unlike her]*

*[when she speaks, it's quieter, slower]*

I think I... 

*[swallows]*

I think I'd find a way to ruin it. Or — not ruin it, that's too dramatic — but I'd find it... unbearable somehow. Like I wouldn't trust it. I'd be waiting for it to stop. And the waiting would be so loud that I couldn't actually just — *receive* it.

*[small, strange laugh, different from the earlier ones — less performance in it]*

God, that's — 

*[stops]*

Twenty years of — I've been chasing this thing and I'm sitting here realizing I've also been making sure I never actually had to *have* it.

*[looks slightly stunned, not performing stunned, actually stunned]*

Because if someone just — loved me. Openly. Without me having to earn it. I don't — my body doesn't know what to do with that. It just... 

*[voice gets smaller]*

It feels like falling. Like there's nothing to hold onto.
